message:
feat(cli): add handoff ledger command; add ensemble conflict/coverage analysis

Handoff CLI additions:
- Add "ntm handoff ledger [session]" command to display continuity
  ledger entries stored at .ntm/ledgers/CONTINUITY_{session}.md
- Support --json flag for machine-readable output
- Default to "general" session when no name provided

Ensemble analysis modules:
- conflicts.go: ConflictTracker for aggregating disagreements between
  mode outputs, ConflictDensity for summarizing conflict frequency
  across mode pairs, ConflictSeverity enum for categorization
- coverage.go: Coverage analysis for ensemble mode output completeness
  tracking and gap identification

Both modules support the synthesis pipeline by providing structured
analysis of multi-mode reasoning outputs.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

End-to-end visual regression tests using VHS (terminal recording) to verify the dashboard renders correctly across different scenarios.

## Visual Comparison Script
\`\`\`bash
#!/bin/bash
# scripts/visual-regression.sh

set -euo pipefail

TESTDATA="testdata"
GOLDEN="$TESTDATA/golden"
SCREENSHOTS="$TESTDATA/screenshots"
THRESHOLD=100  # Maximum pixel difference allowed

echo "Running VHS tapes..."
for tape in $TESTDATA/vhs/*.tape; do
    echo "  Running: $tape"
    vhs "$tape" 2>&1 | tee -a vhs.log
done

echo "Comparing screenshots..."
FAILED=0
for golden in $GOLDEN/*.png; do
    name=$(basename "$golden")
    screenshot="$SCREENSHOTS/$name"
    
    if [[ ! -f "$screenshot" ]]; then
        echo "  MISSING: $name"
        FAILED=1
        continue
    fi
    
    # Compare with ImageMagick
    diff=$(compare -metric AE "$golden" "$screenshot" /dev/null 2>&1 || true)
    
    if [[ "$diff" -gt "$THRESHOLD" ]]; then
        echo "  FAIL: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
        # Generate diff image for debugging
        compare "$golden" "$screenshot" "$TESTDATA/diff-$name" || true
        FAILED=1
    else
        echo "  PASS: $name (diff: $diff pixels)"
    fi
done

if [[ "$FAILED" -eq 1 ]]; then
    echo ""
    echo "Visual regression tests FAILED"
    echo "Check testdata/diff-*.png for differences"
    exit 1
fi

echo ""
echo "All visual regression tests PASSED"
\`\`\`

## E2E Test Wrapper
\`\`\`go
// tests/e2e/dashboard_visual_test.go
func TestDashboard_VisualRegression(t *testing.T) {
    if os.Getenv("RUN_VISUAL_TESTS") == "" {
        t.Skip("set RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 to run visual regression tests")
    }
    
    // Run visual regression script
    cmd := exec.Command("bash", "scripts/visual-regression.sh")
    cmd.Dir = projectRoot()
    output, err := cmd.CombinedOutput()
    
    t.Log(string(output))
    
    if err != nil {
        t.Fatalf("visual regression failed: %v", err)
    }
}
\`\`\`

## Specific Assertions

### No Status Bar Duplication
Check that screenshots contain exactly one instance of status bar text.

### Proper Layout at All Sizes
- 80x24: Compact mode, essential elements visible
- 120x40: Standard mode, all panels visible
- 200x60: Wide mode, expanded layout

### Animation Stability
Multiple screenshots at same state should be visually identical (except for animations).

## CI Integration

visual-tests:

    - name: Upload diff images

      with:

        path: testdata/diff-*.png
\`\`\`

UPDATE_GOLDEN=1 RUN_VISUAL_TESTS=1 go test -v ./tests/e2e/...

# Or manually:
cp testdata/screenshots/*.png testdata/golden/
\`\`\`

 {"id":"bd-13rct","title":"Task: Implement EarlyStopDetector for marginal utility stopping","description":"# Task: EarlyStopDetector Implementation

Stop ensemble when new findings rate drops below threshold.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/early_stop.go
type EarlyStopConfig struct {
    MinAgentsBeforeStop int     // Run at least N agents first
    FindingsThreshold   float64 // Stop if findings/token < threshold
    SimilarityThreshold float64 // Stop if output similarity > threshold
    WindowSize          int     // Evaluate over last N agents
    Enabled             bool
}

type EarlyStopDetector struct {
    Config        EarlyStopConfig
    Outputs       []ModeOutput
    TokensSpent   []int
}

type StopDecision struct {
    ShouldStop    bool
    Reason        string
    FindingsRate  float64
    SimilarityScore float64
    AgentsRun     int
}

func NewEarlyStopDetector(cfg EarlyStopConfig) *EarlyStopDetector
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) RecordOutput(output ModeOutput, tokens int)
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) ShouldStop() StopDecision
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateFindingsRate() float64
func (d *EarlyStopDetector) CalculateSimilarity() float64
```

## Stopping Criteria

### Findings Rate Drop
```
Rate = (new unique findings in window) / (tokens spent in window)
Stop if: rate < threshold AND agents_run >= min_agents
```

### Output Similarity
```
Similarity = average pairwise similarity of outputs in window
Stop if: similarity > threshold (outputs are redundant)
```

## Logging
- Log each decision with metrics
- Log when approaching threshold
- Log final stop reason

Keyboard navigation hints ("↑↓ navigate", "1-9 select", "z zoom", etc.) appear multiple times in the dashboard footer area due to content overflow.

The RenderHelpBar function itself is correctly implemented:
- Uses lipgloss.Width() for width calculations (line 93-101)
- Returns a fresh string each call

Track insights per token spent for efficiency analysis.

## Implementation
```go
// internal/ensemble/velocity.go
type VelocityTracker struct {
    Entries []VelocityEntry
}

type VelocityEntry struct {
    ModeID        string
    ModeName      string
    TokensSpent   int
    FindingsCount int
    UniqueFindings int
    Velocity      float64 // findings per 1k tokens
}

type VelocityReport struct {
    Overall        float64 // total unique findings / total tokens * 1000
    PerMode        []VelocityEntry
    HighPerformers []string // modes above average
    LowPerformers  []string // modes below threshold
    Suggestions    []string
}

func NewVelocityTracker() *VelocityTracker
func (v *VelocityTracker) RecordOutput(modeID string, output ModeOutput, tokens int)
func (v *VelocityTracker) CalculateVelocity() *VelocityReport
func (v *VelocityTracker) Render() string
```

## Velocity Calculation
```
velocity = (unique_findings / tokens_spent) * 1000

Unique findings are de-duplicated by:
- normalized finding text (lowercase + trim)
- evidence_pointer when present
```

## Inputs
- Token counts from BudgetTracker / TokenEstimator (per-mode)
- Outputs from normalized ModeOutput

## Edge Cases
- tokens_spent == 0 → skip or mark velocity as 0 with warning
- empty findings → velocity 0

## Visualization

```
Per Mode:

L3 Meta-cognitive:      2.5 findings/1k
H2 Strategic:           1.9 findings/1k

Suggestion: K1 underperforming, consider early stop
```

## Logging Requirements

- Log threshold crossings (below min velocity)

## Tests

 {"id":"bd-150ij","title":"AgentLauncher: Handle window:pane addressing (session:1.N format)","description":"## Purpose
Set working directory to the assigned project path before launching each agent, ensuring agents operate in the correct context.

## Implementation Details

```go
// PaneLauncher handles agent launching with directory setup
type PaneLauncher struct {
    tmux          *tmux.Client
    cmdBuilder    *LaunchCommandBuilder
    orchestrator  *session.SessionOrchestrator
    logger        *slog.Logger
}

// LaunchAgentInPane sets up and launches an agent in a specific pane
func (pl *PaneLauncher) LaunchAgentInPane(ctx context.Context, sessionName string, paneSpec PaneSpec) error {
    // Validate project path exists
    if _, err := os.Stat(paneSpec.Project); err != nil {
        pl.logger.Error("project path does not exist",
            "project", paneSpec.Project,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("project path %s: %w", paneSpec.Project, err)
    }
    
    pl.logger.Info("preparing pane for agent launch",
        "session", sessionName,
        "pane_index", paneSpec.Index,
        "project", paneSpec.Project,
        "agent_type", paneSpec.AgentType)
    
    // Get pane ID from orchestrator
    paneID, err := pl.orchestrator.GetPaneID(sessionName, paneSpec.Index)
    if err != nil {
        return fmt.Errorf("get pane ID: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Step 1: Change to project directory
    cdCmd := fmt.Sprintf("cd %q", paneSpec.Project)
    if err := pl.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, cdCmd, true); err != nil {
        pl.logger.Error("failed to cd to project",
            "pane", paneID,
            "project", paneSpec.Project,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("cd to project: %w", err)
    }
    
    pl.logger.Debug("changed directory",
        "pane", paneID,
        "project", paneSpec.Project)
    
    // Brief pause to ensure cd completes
    time.Sleep(100 * time.Millisecond)
    
    // Step 2: Build and send launch command
    launchCmd := pl.cmdBuilder.BuildLaunchCommand(paneSpec, paneSpec.Project)
    shellCmd := launchCmd.ToShellCommand()
    
    if err := pl.tmux.SendKeys(paneID, shellCmd, true); err != nil {
        pl.logger.Error("failed to launch agent",
            "pane", paneID,
            "command", shellCmd,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("launch agent: %w", err)
    }
    
    pl.logger.Info("agent launched successfully",
        "session", sessionName,
        "pane", paneID,
        "agent_type", paneSpec.AgentType,
        "project", filepath.Base(paneSpec.Project),
        "command", shellCmd)
    
    return nil
}
```

## Integration with Existing Code
- Use internal/session.SessionOrchestrator for pane management
- Use internal/tmux.Client for SendKeys

## Logging Requirements
- Log project path validation
- Log directory change step
- Log launch command execution
- Include pane ID and session name in all logs

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Validate project path exists before launch
- [ ] Change directory before agent launch
- [ ] Small delay between cd and launch for reliability
- [ ] Quote paths to handle spaces
- [ ] Comprehensive error logging
- [ ] Integration with SessionOrchestrator

```go
package caut

type UsagePoller struct {
    config   *config.CautConfig
    cache    *UsageCache
    interval time.Duration
    stopCh   chan struct{}
    alertCh  chan Alert
}

func (p *UsagePoller) Start() {
    go p.pollLoop()
}

func (p *UsagePoller) pollLoop() {
    ticker := time.NewTicker(p.interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()
    
    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            usage, err := p.fetchUsage()
            if err != nil {
                // Log error, continue
                continue
            }
            p.cache.Update(usage)
            p.checkAlerts(usage)
        case <-p.stopCh:
            return
        }
    }
}

func (p *UsagePoller) fetchUsage() (*Usage, error) {
    cmd := exec.Command("caut", "status", "--json")
    // Parse output
}
```

```go
func (m *DashboardModel) renderAgentStatus(pane string) string {
    state := m.healthMonitor.GetState(pane)
    
    switch state.Classification {
    case "useful", "waiting":
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("green")).Render("🟢")
    case "idle":
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("yellow")).Render("🟡")
    case "stuck":
        return lipgloss.NewStyle().Foreground(lipgloss.Color("red")).Render("🔴")
    default:
        return "⚫"
    }
}
```

 {"id":"bd-3lo54","title":"EPIC: Weighted Multi-Project Agent Swarm Orchestration","description":"## Overview

This epic introduces a fully automated multi-project agent swarm orchestration system that intelligently allocates coding agents (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini) across multiple projects based on their open bead backlogs.

## Problem Statement

When managing multiple projects in /dp, each with varying amounts of work (tracked via beads), manually calculating agent allocation, creating tmux sessions, launching agents, and handling usage limits is tedious and error-prone. We need a single command that:

1. Scans projects for open bead counts
2. Calculates weighted agent allocation (more beads = more agents)
3. Creates tmux sessions with correct pane geometry
4. Launches agents (cc, cod, gmi) in each pane
5. Sends marching orders to all agents
6. Monitors for usage limit hits
7. Auto-respawns with account rotation when limits hit

## Key Features

### 1. Bead-Weighted Allocation
- Scan /dp projects (or specified list) for open bead counts via `br list --status open --json`
- Apply tiered allocation formula:
  - 400+ beads: 4 cc, 4 cod, 2 gmi agents
  - 100-399 beads: 3 cc, 3 cod, 2 gmi agents
  - <100 beads: 1 cc, 1 cod, 1 gmi agents
- Configurable thresholds and ratios

### 2. Session Orchestration
- Create 3 sessions per agent type: {type}_agents_1, {type}_agents_2, {type}_agents_3
- Distribute panes evenly across sessions of same type
- Ensure identical pane geometry across sessions
- Support remote execution via SSH

### 3. Usage Limit Detection
- Monitor pane output for limit patterns:
  - CC: "You've hit your limit"
  - COD: "You've hit your usage limit"
- Configurable pattern matching
- Real-time monitoring via background goroutines

### 4. Auto-Respawn with Account Rotation
- When limit detected: kill agent, rotate to next account, respawn
- Integration with caam (coding_agent_account_manager) for account switching
- Configurable retry limits and backoff

### 5. Marching Orders Injection
- Send standardized initial prompt to all agents
- Staggered sending to avoid rate limits
- Codex double-Enter quirk handling

## Technical Approach

### New CLI Command: `ntm swarm`

```bash
# Basic usage - scan /dp and auto-allocate
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp

# Specify projects explicitly
ntm swarm --projects=flywheel_connectors,ntm,asupersync

# Custom allocation formula
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp --tier1-threshold=400 --tier2-threshold=100

# Dry run to preview allocation
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp --dry-run

# With account rotation enabled
ntm swarm --scan-dir=/dp --auto-rotate-accounts

# Robot mode for automation
ntm --robot-swarm --scan-dir=/dp
```

### New Internal Packages

1. `internal/swarm/` - Core orchestration logic
2. `internal/beadscanner/` - Project bead counting
3. `internal/limitdetect/` - Usage limit pattern matching
4. `internal/accountrotate/` - Account rotation integration

### Configuration

New config section in `config.toml`:

```toml
[swarm]
default_scan_dir = "/dp"
tier1_threshold = 400  # beads
tier2_threshold = 100
tier1_allocation = { cc = 4, cod = 4, gmi = 2 }
tier2_allocation = { cc = 3, cod = 3, gmi = 2 }
tier3_allocation = { cc = 1, cod = 1, gmi = 1 }
sessions_per_type = 3
stagger_delay_ms = 300
auto_rotate_accounts = false

[swarm.limit_patterns]
cc = ["You've hit your limit", "usage limit"]
cod = ["You've hit your usage limit"]
gmi = ["quota exceeded"]

[swarm.marching_orders]
default = "First read ALL of the AGENTS.md file..."
review = "I want you to sort of randomly explore..."
```

## Success Criteria

1. Single command spawns weighted agent swarm across projects
2. Agents auto-start with marching orders
3. Usage limits detected within 30 seconds
4. Auto-respawn works with account rotation
5. Robot mode outputs JSON for automation
6. Full integration with existing NTM features (health, resilience, agent-mail)

## Dependencies

- Existing: spawn, send, health, resilience, config, tmux packages
- External: br (beads_rust), caam (coding_agent_account_manager)

## Out of Scope (for this epic)

- GUI/TUI for swarm management (future enhancement)
- Cloud-based account rotation (use local caam)
- Cross-machine swarm coordination (single machine focus)","status":"open","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","created_at":"2026-01-22T06:56:54.050264582Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-26T00:38:02.407074656Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-3lo54","depends_on_id":"bd-1bdxp","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-28T04:13:06Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-3mebp","title":"Task: Add concurrency safety to ntm assign watch and completion detection","description":"## Overview
Ensure thread-safety in watch mode and completion detection to prevent race conditions.

## Identified Race Conditions

### 1. Simultaneous Completion Events
Multiple agents complete at nearly the same time:
```
Time    Event
0ms     Agent 2 completes bd-abc
5ms     Agent 3 completes bd-def
10ms    Handler for bd-abc starts
15ms    Handler for bd-def starts  <-- Race!
```

**Solution:**
```go
type WatchLoop struct {
    completionCh   chan CompletionEvent
    assignmentLock sync.Mutex
}

func (w *WatchLoop) handleCompletions() {
    for event := range w.completionCh {
        w.assignmentLock.Lock()
        w.processCompletion(event)
        w.assignmentLock.Unlock()
    }
}
```

### 2. Assignment Store Updates During Query
While querying assignment store, another goroutine updates it:
```
Goroutine 1: Reading assignments to find idle agents
Goroutine 2: Updating assignment status to "completed"
```

**Solution:**
```go
// Already have mutex in AssignmentStore, ensure all methods use it:
func (s *AssignmentStore) GetIdleAgents() []Agent {
    s.mutex.RLock()
    defer s.mutex.RUnlock()
    // ... read logic
}

func (s *AssignmentStore) UpdateStatus(beadID string, status AssignmentStatus) {
    s.mutex.Lock()
    defer s.mutex.Unlock()
    // ... write logic
}
```

### 3. BV Cache Invalidation During Read
BV cache invalidated while strategy is reading recommendations:
```
Goroutine 1: Strategy reading from cache
Goroutine 2: Completion triggers cache invalidation
```

**Solution:**
```go
type BVClient struct {
    cache      *RecommendationCache
    cacheMutex sync.RWMutex
}

func (c *BVClient) GetRecommendations() []Recommendation {
    c.cacheMutex.RLock()
    cached := c.cache.Get()
    c.cacheMutex.RUnlock()
    
    if cached != nil {
        return cached
    }
    
    // Fetch fresh, then cache with write lock
    fresh := c.fetchFromBV()
    c.cacheMutex.Lock()
    c.cache.Set(fresh)
    c.cacheMutex.Unlock()
    return fresh
}
```

### 4. Completion Detection Race
Multiple detection methods find the same completion:
```
Method 1 (bead_poll): Detects completion
Method 2 (pattern_match): Also detects completion
Both try to emit CompletionEvent
```

**Solution:**
```go
type CompletionDetector struct {
    detected map[string]time.Time  // bead_id -> detected_at
    dedupMu  sync.Mutex
    dedupTTL time.Duration  // 5s default
}

func (d *CompletionDetector) emitIfNew(event CompletionEvent) bool {
    d.dedupMu.Lock()
    defer d.dedupMu.Unlock()
    
    if prev, ok := d.detected[event.BeadID]; ok {
        if time.Since(prev) < d.dedupTTL {
            return false  // Duplicate
        }
    }
    d.detected[event.BeadID] = time.Now()
    return true
}
```

### 5. File Reservation Transfer During Reassignment
Old reservation released, but new reservation fails:
```
1. Release old reservation  [SUCCESS]
2. Create new reservation   [FAIL - timeout]
3. Now no reservation exists
```

**Solution:**
```go
func transferReservation(from, to Agent, paths []string) error {
    // Create new reservation FIRST
    newRes, err := createReservation(to, paths)
    if err != nil {
        return err  // Old reservation still intact
    }
    
    // Then release old
    releaseReservation(from, paths)
    return nil
}
```

End-to-end test for the complete swarm lifecycle: scan projects, calculate allocations, create sessions, spawn agents, and shutdown.

## Test Script

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# e2e_swarm_lifecycle.sh
set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-SWARM]"
TEST_DIR="/tmp/ntm_e2e_swarm_$$"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0

log() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX $(date +%H:%M:%S) $*"; }
pass() { log "PASS: $1"; ((PASSED++)); }
fail() { log "FAIL: $1"; ((FAILED++)); }

cleanup() {
    log "Cleaning up..."
    # Kill any test sessions
    tmux list-sessions 2>/dev/null | grep "ntm_e2e_" | cut -d: -f1 | xargs -r -I{} tmux kill-session -t {}
    rm -rf "$TEST_DIR"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log "Starting Swarm Lifecycle E2E tests"
log "Test directory: $TEST_DIR"

# Setup: Create test projects with beads
log "Setup: Creating test projects"
mkdir -p "$TEST_DIR/proj1/.beads" "$TEST_DIR/proj2/.beads"

# Create issues.jsonl for proj1 (10 open beads = Tier 1)
for i in $(seq 1 10); do
    echo '{"id":"p1-'$i'","title":"Task '$i'","status":"open"}' >> "$TEST_DIR/proj1/.beads/issues.jsonl"
done
log "Created proj1 with 10 open beads"

# Create issues.jsonl for proj2 (3 open beads = Tier 3)
for i in $(seq 1 3); do
    echo '{"id":"p2-'$i'","title":"Task '$i'","status":"open"}' >> "$TEST_DIR/proj2/.beads/issues.jsonl"
done
log "Created proj2 with 3 open beads"

# Test 1: Scan projects
log "Test 1: Scan projects for bead counts"
SCAN_OUTPUT=$(ntm swarm --scan-dir "$TEST_DIR" --dry-run --json 2>&1 || echo "{}")
log "Scan output: $SCAN_OUTPUT"

if echo "$SCAN_OUTPUT" | grep -q "proj1"; then
    pass "Project scan found proj1"
else
    fail "Project scan missing proj1"
fi

# Test 2: Verify allocation calculation
log "Test 2: Allocation calculation"
ALLOCATIONS=$(echo "$SCAN_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.allocations // []' 2>/dev/null || echo "[]")
log "Allocations: $ALLOCATIONS"

PROJ1_AGENTS=$(echo "$ALLOCATIONS" | jq -r '.[] | select(.project.name=="proj1") | .total_agents' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
log "proj1 total agents: $PROJ1_AGENTS"

if [[ "$PROJ1_AGENTS" -gt "0" ]]; then
    pass "Allocation calculated for proj1"
else
    fail "No allocation for proj1"
fi

# Test 3: Create sessions (without launching real agents)
log "Test 3: Session creation (dry-run)"
SESSION_COUNT=$(echo "$SCAN_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.sessions | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "0")
log "Session count: $SESSION_COUNT"

if [[ "$SESSION_COUNT" -gt "0" ]]; then
    pass "Sessions planned"
else
    fail "No sessions planned"
fi

# Test 4: Verify session naming
log "Test 4: Session naming convention"
SESSIONS=$(echo "$SCAN_OUTPUT" | jq -r '.sessions[].name' 2>/dev/null || echo "")
log "Session names: $SESSIONS"

for session in $SESSIONS; do
    if [[ "$session" =~ ^(cc|cod|gmi)_agents_[0-9]+$ ]]; then
        log "  Valid session name: $session"
    else
        fail "Invalid session name: $session"
    fi
done
pass "Session naming convention"

# Test 5: Create a real test session (minimal)
log "Test 5: Real session creation"
TEST_SESSION="ntm_e2e_lifecycle_$$"
tmux new-session -d -s "$TEST_SESSION" -x 120 -y 30
sleep 0.5

if tmux has-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null; then
    log "Session $TEST_SESSION created"
    pass "Real session creation"
else
    fail "Failed to create real session"
fi

# Test 6: Pane creation
log "Test 6: Pane creation"
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -v
tmux split-window -t "$TEST_SESSION" -h
sleep 0.5

PANE_COUNT=$(tmux list-panes -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null | wc -l)
log "Pane count: $PANE_COUNT"

if [[ "$PANE_COUNT" -ge "3" ]]; then
    pass "Multiple panes created"
else
    fail "Pane creation failed (expected 3, got $PANE_COUNT)"
fi

# Test 7: Graceful shutdown
log "Test 7: Graceful shutdown"
tmux kill-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null || true

if ! tmux has-session -t "$TEST_SESSION" 2>/dev/null; then
    pass "Graceful shutdown"
else
    fail "Session not killed"
fi

# Summary
log "=========================================="
log "E2E Swarm Lifecycle Tests Complete"
log "Passed: $PASSED"
log "Failed: $FAILED"
log "=========================================="

[[ $FAILED -eq 0 ]] && exit 0 || exit 1
```
